description:
You are an urban planner evaluating land use diversity across San Francisco zones using the UrbanSim dataset at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5. Quantify the land use mix by computing the Shannon entropy and normalized entropy of building types for each zone using the buildings and parcels tables. Identify the top 5 most diverse and top 5 least diverse zones, considering only those with at least 10 total buildings.

Save the analysis in a Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/land_use_entropy.ipynb with all cells executed. Export the zone-level results to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_entropy.csv with columns: zone_id, entropy, normalized_entropy, dominant_type, num_building_types, and total_buildings. Save a horizontal bar chart of sorted normalized entropy by zone to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/entropy_barplot.png. Generate a summary at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/entropy_summary.json containing the most_diverse_zones, least_diverse_zones, mean_normalized_entropy, median_normalized_entropy, and num_zones_analyzed.
